commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=02f5ff99d2dce9e72a93f3e4351675cf9af986b7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR converts gateway service doctor repairs and adjacent structured doctor buckets to health-check repairs with service effects, note routing, dry-run/diff support, and focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: return `serviceRewriteBlocked: true` with remaining config issues, and the structured repair reports an update effect after the legacy helper returns before service mutation.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer and contributor handling because the PR is protected, stacked, lacks live service-manager proof, and has a concrete repair-output correctness bug.

target.title: Doctor: structure gateway service repairs
target.body:
## Goal

Convert gateway service maintenance to structured doctor repairs while keeping service-manager work represented as service effects, not fake file/config diffs.

Gateway service repair is inherently platform/service-manager work. This PR’s goal is to make that work detectable, previewable, note-aware, and validateable through the structured contract without pretending launchd/systemd operations are config patches.

## Scope

| Check | Goal | Behavior |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `core/doctor/gateway-services/extra` | Detect and clean up legacy or gateway-like services from the structured path. | Detect is silent; repair delegates to `repairExtraGatewayServices(...)`. |
| `core/doctor/gateway-services/config` | Detect and repair service config drift from the structured path. | Detect is silent; repair delegates to `repairGatewayServiceConfig(...)`. |
| `core/doctor/gateway-services/platform-notes` | Preserve operator context without detect-time printing. | Repair notes flow through `DoctorPrompter.note(...)` into ordered repair notes. |

## Structured Repair Matrix

| Check | Detect / lint behavior | Repair behavior | Dry-run / effects | Note / validation proof |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `core/doctor/gateway-services/extra` | Detects gateway-like/legacy services without printing. | Converted repair calls `repairExtraGatewayServices(...)`; legacy `maybeScanExtraGatewayServices(...)` delegates for old contribution paths. | `would-remove-legacy-gateway-service` and `remove-legacy-gateway-service` service effects. | Detect-after verifies removed legacy services are no longer reported in focused tests. |
| `core/doctor/gateway-services/config` | Detects service config drift silently. | Converted repair calls `repairGatewayServiceConfig(...)`; legacy `maybeRepairGatewayServiceConfig(...)` delegates for old contribution paths. | `would-update-gateway-service-config` and `update-gateway-service-config` service effects. | Focused tests cover repair, dry-run, effects, and validation. |
| Gateway platform notes | Platform/service caveats are structured findings or repair notes, not detect-time prints. | Timing-sensitive repair presentation goes through shared `DoctorPrompter.note(...)`. | No synthetic config/file diffs for service-manager work. | Structured adapter routes notes into ordered `repairNotes`. |
| Install-plan warnings | Suppressed during detect/audit and lint. | Emitted during actual repair when the install plan is built for mutation. | Side-effect metadata stays explicit. | Tests cover silent detect/lint behavior and repair-time note routing. |

## Proof

| Proof | Result |
| --- | --- |
| Head | `02f5ff99d2dc` on `doctor-gateway-services-structured-repairs`, stacked on #84326, #84290, and #83753. |
| Focused gateway/core/repair/lint tests | `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-daemon-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed, 6 files / 115 tests. |
| Core typecheck | `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo` passed. |
| Formatting / whitespace | `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...` on touched gateway/core files and `git diff --check doctor-sandbox-structured-repairs..HEAD` passed. |
| Autoreview | `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-daemon-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot"` passed with no accepted/actionable findings. |

## Out of Scope

- Live launchd/systemd mutation proof for this stacked branch.
- Changing gateway service policy or install semantics.
- Emitting synthetic file diffs for service-manager operations.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Gateway service doctor repairs now run through structured health checks at their original doctor contribution position, with silent detect/lint behavior, ordered repair presentation, detect-after validation, and concrete side-effect metadata.

Real environment tested: WSL source worktree `/tmp/openclaw-doctor-next` on restacked branch head `02f5ff99d2dc`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused gateway service, daemon-flow, core check, health contribution, repair-flow, and lint-flow tests; core tsgo; touched-file formatting; whitespace check; autoreview with parallel focused tests.

Evidence after fix: Focused test output showed 6 files / 115 tests passing. Core tsgo, formatting, whitespace, and autoreview passed.

Observed result after fix: structured gateway repairs report service effects, preserve note ordering through the shared repair-note contract, keep detect/lint silent, and validate repaired findings.

What was not tested: live launchd/systemd install. This proof uses mocked service managers and command helpers.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
